description: The Competition Commission of India has cleared twelve Delhi hospitals of overcharging patients. The ruling has sparked debate about consumer protection in India's vast healthcare sector.

description: Sachin Pilot downplayed Ashok Gehlot's recent criticism, emphasizing party unity and affection. Pilot highlighted national challenges like exam paper leaks and rising prices, vowing to strengthen the INDIA bloc against the BJP. He also criticized the Rajasthan government and alleged institutional pressure on the Election Commission.

title: Akshay Kumar dismisses Welcome To The Jungle salary rumour; opens up about retirement
Buyer claims Rs 1.65 crore Defender failed its speed claim; court orders full refund
description: A luxury car buyer has won a major consumer dispute against Jaguar Land Rover India. The company was ordered to refund ₹1.65 crore with interest for a defective Defender SUV. The vehicle failed to meet advertised performance, had missing features, and underwent unauthorized chassis modifications, leading to the commission's strong ruling.

title: 'By next World Cup, India must have a backup': Raina on Pandya injuries
description: Suresh Raina has urged India to find a dependable backup for all-rounder Hardik Pandya, citing his persistent fitness issues. Pandya's recent injury, ruling him out of the Afghanistan series, underscores the team's need for depth in pace-bowling all-rounders ahead of the next ODI World Cup. Identifying and nurturing an alternative is crucial to avoid vulnerability.

title: Trump says US secretly moved 100 million barrels of oil past Iran. Reports suggest it wasn’t a secret
'US navy involved, attacks must end': MEA protests repeated strikes on ships with Indians in Hormuz
Dubai-based Indian banker conquers Mount Everest after life-changing accident
description: At 40, Shradha Gupta wasn't a mountaineer but a banking executive. After a life-altering accident, she stopped waiting for the 'right time' and began climbing, reaching Everest just three years later. Her journey proves ambition isn't finite and extraordinary achievements start with a single step.

title: In Your Neighbourhood: The last camera shop of Chor Bazaar
Noida Airport flights to begin next week: Check details on parking, transport options
Yamaha Saluto RX shows defects within 30 days of purchase, court asks maker to refund or replace bike
IT payers, dead women receiving Gruha Lakshmi benefits: Karnataka to introduce biometric verification
Boost for litchi exports! Assam’s GI-tagged Tezpur litchi reaches Dubai, Singapore
description: Assam's prized Tezpur litchi has made its international debut with the first export consignment reaching Dubai. This milestone, facilitated by APEDA, highlights efforts to boost Northeast agricultural exports. The GI-tagged fruit, celebrated for its exceptional taste and aroma, fetched farmers higher prices, promising enhanced livelihoods and opening new global markets for this century-old cultivation legacy.

title: Haryana guest teacher suspended days after joining Cockroach Janta Party protest
description: A guest teacher in Haryana's Rohtak district has been suspended after participating in a protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. Sulekha Dalal's suspension, effective June 8, followed a viral video of her addressing the Cockroach Janta Party rally. Dalal stated her participation was due to concerns over recruitment exam irregularities affecting her son.

title: 46 international students receive MTech degrees at IIT Madras’ Zanzibar campus
‘Totally baseless’: Cong rejects Mamata merger; accuses BJP of 'seat-chori' over TMC exodus
‘I only got 50% back’: Veteran financier slams RJ Balaji over Suriya film Karuppu row
Over 20% returns: How NRIs could make a killing after banks hike FCNR(B) deposit rates
How ‘Maa Behen’ reclaims the messy woman
‘What’ll we feed children?’: Farmers oppose land acquisition for Shaktipeeth Expressway
Karnataka High Court relocation: Government eyes Bengaluru Turf Club site for new complex
‘We were called ‘chor’ because of him’: TMC MP targets Abhishek Banerjee
Bengaluru techie identifies stressful colleagues; Whoop tracker experiment goes viral
MT Jalveer comes under attack off Oman coast; over 20 sailors on board
description: India is closely monitoring a maritime incident near Oman's Shinas port, following recent attacks on commercial vessels. This comes after three Indian sailors died in a US-led strike on another ship. The Indian Embassy in Muscat is coordinating with local authorities for details, while New Delhi has lodged a strong protest with the US over the earlier incident.

title: Rahul Dravid’s son Anvay named in India Under-19 squad for Sri Lanka tour
After Samay Raina, Rs 370-biryani raises the same question about punishment
Bombay High Court upholds RBI employee’s compulsory retirement over nearly 3 years of unauthorised absence
Rs 370 biryani row: Founder says fired Himanshu Jangra after ‘two clients stepped back’
'Will not leave Mamata in difficult time': Shatrughan Sinha picks Team Didi amid TMC turmoil
description: Amidst a party exodus, TMC MP Shatrughan Sinha pledged loyalty to Mamata Banerjee, stating he wouldn't abandon her during challenging times, recalling her support for him. This declaration follows reports of his alleged involvement in forming a separate faction. Earlier, Sinha also congratulated Prime Minister Narendra Modi on his 12 years in office, calling him a "friend and guide."

title: Aditi Mittal, Rohan Joshi on Rs 370 biryani row: ‘Humiliating women is male bonding’
Mumbai’s Metro 3 goes online: Why underground mobile coverage is still incomplete
'Choose Abhishek or me': Kalyan Banerjee's blunt message to Mamata amid turmoil in TMC
description: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee issued an ultimatum to Mamata Banerjee, demanding she choose between her nephew Abhishek Banerjee and senior party workers. Banerjee also announced he would no longer represent Abhishek in legal matters, citing disrespect and arrogance from the latter. This comes amid internal party tensions following electoral setbacks.

title: Third Trinamool Rajya Sabha MP resigns: Who is Prakash Chik Baraik?
Imtiaz Ali says Bobby Deol still brings up being replaced in Jab We Met: ‘Every time we speak’
America’s message for the World Cup: Stay away
An expert explains the motivations behind Xi Jinping’s North Korea visit, in a changing Asia
Pranit More defended his crowd work as ‘talking to friends’: ‘Ashleel show got most views’

Valet damages car, makes owner wait 45 minutes: Restobar ordered to pay Rs 3 lakh compensation
UP Police Constable exam ends; forged documents, impersonation cases reported
MHT CET 2026 result for BHMCT, BCA, BBA, BMS, BSc nursing and other courses declared
The civilisational trap in the Indian Knowledge Systems debate

Karnataka High Court calls for homestay policy, says law doesn’t cover all apsects
‘Negotiate with bombs’: Hegseth defends US strikes on Iran’s Bandar Abbas, Qeshm as Tehran closes Strait of Hormuz
‘What ideology is this?’: MP Amol Kolhe’s Savitribai Phule serial axed; Rohit Pawar offers Rs 50 lakh lifeline
IIM Lucknow opens BS in AI and Business Analytics for JEE Advanced qualifiers; apply by July 3
Palantir CEO’s message to tech CEO: Don’t be surprised if your employees turn against you
description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp cautions tech leaders against publicizing AI-driven job cuts, warning it could provoke backlash from employees, voters, and policymakers. He suggests such pronouncements risk fueling opposition and strengthening calls for tech regulation. Karp himself acknowledges Palantir uses AI for efficiency but plans to boost revenue without significant workforce expansion.

title: Is Tanvi Sharma’s game developing more like Saina Nehwal’s than PV Sindhu’s?

Maharashtra farmers skip dry sowing amid monsoon delay

Punjab man dies in accident avoiding dog, court orders 4 insurance firms to pay Rs 1.75 crore to sons
NCDRC rejects insurer’s appeal, orders Rs 5 lakh mediclaim payout for angioplasty
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi told to stay away from professional managers
description: Former India pacer S Sreesanth has lauded teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who recently became the youngest player selected for an Indian men's team. Sreesanth emphasized Suryavanshi's commitment and perseverance, urging the 15-year-old to focus solely on cricket and avoid distractions from professional managers, trusting his family to handle off-field matters.

title: Scientists develop smart bandage that detects infections and releases antibiotics when needed
Lock Upp teaser: Jailers Farah Khan and Riteish Deshmukh want inmates to ‘own’ their truth

Did Israel violate international laws in Lebanon? UN to probe next week
‘You know Salman Khan’: Lawrence Bishnoi gang fires at Guru Randhawa’s Delhi gym

Snapchat rolls out friends-only Stories and Spotlight profiles for users under 16
Wanted in murder, extortion cases, Haryana gangster Venkat Garg extradited from Georgia
Kerala reports suspected Nipah virus case — trader whose godown had fruit bats
Punjab doctor 'who helped fund' husband's hospital found dead
description: A doctor's husband, an eye surgeon, faces charges of abetting suicide after his wife was found dead. Her parents allege extramarital affairs, violence, and a massive Rs 2.5 crore loan taken in her name without consent. The couple had been living separately following alleged assault attempts and threats.

title: Bentley, G-Wagon, sex parties: Inside Indian-American Mahender Makhijani’s $100 million scam
Virat Kohli refuses to pose as daughter Vamika waits for him: ‘Respect his privacy’
Giant fan-shaped structure hidden below Antarctica’s ice, scientists find
Microsoft warns staff: Don't touch Claude Fable 5, lawyers are still reading fine print
description: Microsoft has blocked its own employees from using Anthropic's newly launched Claude Fable 5 inside GitHub Copilot, citing unresolved concerns over a 30-day data retention policy tied to the Mythos-class model. The Verge reports Microsoft's legal team is still reviewing the changes, even as paying Copilot and Foundry customers already have access. Other Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention rules. Anthropic has not offered a workaround.

title: Drunk Delhi cop enters Gurgaon society, assaults woman and threatens to rape her sister
description: A 21-year-old woman was allegedly assaulted by an intoxicated Delhi Police constable in Gurgaon's Sector 56. The accused also threatened her family and issued a rape threat to her younger sister. The constable was arrested and his bail application was dismissed. Police have added POCSO charges. CCTV footage is being examined.

title: How promises of jobs and a better life trapped 453 Nepalese youths in UP
description: Hundreds of Nepalese youths, lured by job promises, were rescued from a sophisticated scam in Uttar Pradesh's Kushinagar.  Victims paid hefty sums for training and placements that never materialized, facing confinement and exploitation.  Alerts to the Nepal Embassy triggered a multi-agency operation, exposing the extensive cross-border racket.
